channel.title: Embedded Systems on Vamsi Kiran | Firmware Engineer
channel.description: Recent content in Embedded Systems on Vamsi Kiran | Firmware Engineer

item.title: STM32F4 Bare-Metal LED Toggle: Bit-Fields & SIL Validation
item.description:
🛠 Technology Stack

  • Hardware: STM32F407VG (ARM Cortex-M4)
  • Language: C (C11/C17)
  • Verification: Renode (Software-in-the-Loop)
  • Key Concepts: C Bit-Fields, MMIO, Register Mapping, SIL Testing

Project Overview

This project is a low-level firmware implementation for the STM32F407 demonstrating peripheral control via direct register manipulation. The primary focus was replacing standard “magic number” bit-masking with type-safe C Bit-Field structures to interface directly with the AHB1 bus matrix.

Technical Highlights

1. Structured Register Mapping (Bit-Fields)

Instead of using manual bit-shifting (e.g., *(GPIO + OFFSET) |= (1 << 12)), I utilized structured bit-fields within C unions and structs.
